title: nono Profile
description: A nono profile definition that configures sandbox capabilities, filesystem access, network policy, and other security settings for running untrusted processes.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for editor validation and autocompletion.

extends.description: Name of a base profile (or array of base profiles) to inherit from. All fields from the base profile are used as defaults, with this profile's values taking precedence. When an array is given, bases are merged left-to-right before the child is applied. Shared transitive bases are deduplicated: each profile is resolved at most once.

meta.description: Profile metadata including name, version, description, and author.

security.description: Security configuration for process-level isolation modes (signal, IPC, process info, capability elevation, WSL2 proxy policy).

groups.description: Group composition: include and exclude policy group names resolved from policy.json.

commands.description: Command allow/deny lists applied during sandbox setup.

filesystem.description: Filesystem access rules specifying which directories and files the sandboxed process can read or write.

network.description: Network access configuration including proxy settings, credential injection, and port allowlists.

diagnostics.description: Diagnostic output controls. These settings do not change sandbox enforcement.

env_credentials.description: Maps keystore account names (or op://, bw://, apple-password://, env:// URIs) to environment variable names. Secrets are loaded from the system keystore at startup.

secrets.description: Alias for env_credentials. Maps keystore account names to environment variable names.

environment.description: Controls which environment variables are passed to the sandboxed process. By default all variables are inherited.

command_policies.description: tool-sandbox command-control policy. tool-sandbox activates only when commands is non-empty; broker-mediated enforcement is available on Linux and macOS, with platform-specific capability limits.

workdir.description: Working directory access configuration controlling whether and how the current working directory is shared with the sandboxed process.

hooks.description: Hook configurations for target applications. Maps application names to their hook definitions.

session_hooks.description: Session lifecycle hooks. Scripts run outside the sandbox with host privileges before and after the sandboxed process.

rollback.description: Rollback snapshot configuration controlling which files are excluded from undo snapshots.

undo.description: Alias for rollback. Controls which files are excluded from undo snapshots.

open_urls.description: Supervisor-delegated URL opening configuration for OAuth2 login flows and similar operations. When present, replaces the base profile's config entirely. When absent or null, inherits from the base profile. To clear inherited permissions, provide an explicit empty object with allow_origins: [] and allow_localhost: false.

allow_launch_services.description: Opt-in gate for temporary direct LaunchServices opens on macOS. Must be paired with the CLI flag --allow-launch-services. Set to null to inherit from the base profile.

allow_gpu.description: Opt-in gate for GPU access (Metal/IOKit on Apple Silicon macOS, render nodes on Linux). Intel Macs are not yet supported. Must be paired with the CLI flag --allow-gpu. Set to null to inherit from the base profile.

interactive.description: Deprecated. Parsed for backward compatibility but ignored. Supervised mode preserves TTY by default.

packs.description: Pack dependencies verified at launch. Each entry is a namespace/name reference to an installed pack.

command_args.description: Extra arguments appended to the child command at launch. Supports variable expansion (e.g. $NONO_CONFIG, $NONO_PACKAGES).

credential_capture.description: Maps logical credential names to supervisor-side commands used by custom_credentials entries with credential_key: "cmd://<name>". Commands are run lazily when the proxy first needs the credential; stdout is injected by the proxy and never exposed to the sandboxed child.

unsafe_macos_seatbelt_rules.description: macOS-only. Expert escape hatch: raw Seatbelt S-expression rules applied verbatim to the sandbox policy. Each entry must be a valid S-expression, e.g. "(allow iokit-open)". Rules are validated at load time and rejected if malformed. Ignored on Linux. Surfaced prominently in 'nono profile show' output. If a rule pattern becomes common, prefer promoting it to a typed first-class capability instead.

version.description: Version string for this profile definition.

description.description: Optional description of what this profile is for.

author.description: Optional author or maintainer of this profile.

CredentialCaptureEntry.description: Configuration for a supervisor-side credential capture command.

command.description: Command and arguments to execute without a shell, for example ["gh", "auth", "token"]. The executable is resolved before proxy startup.

timeout_secs.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the command.

ttl_secs.description: How long to cache the captured credential in memory. Set to 0 to disable caching.

cache_ttl_secs.description: Preferred alias for ttl_secs. How long to cache captured material in memory. Set to 0 to disable caching. Do not set both ttl_secs and cache_ttl_secs.

cache_path_regex.description: Optional regular expression evaluated against the upstream request path. If it matches, capture group 1 is used as the cache scope; otherwise the full match is used. When absent or unmatched, the request host is used.

stdin.description: Controls stdin for the capture command. null closes stdin unless interaction.stdio is true. request_json writes request metadata JSON to stdin.

oneOf.description: Capture stdout as a single credential string.

output.description: Controls how capture stdout is interpreted. JSON output requires an object form with allow_headers.

interaction.description: Explicit opt-in policy for interactive capture commands.

CredentialCaptureOutputConfig.description: Structured output configuration for a credential capture command.

format.description: Interpret stdout as JSON. The supported shape is {"headers": {"Header-Name": "value"}}.

allow_headers.description: Header names that JSON output may inject. Required for json output.

CredentialCaptureInteraction.description: Explicit policy for capture commands that need terminal or browser interaction.

stdio.description: Allow the capture command to use inherited stderr and, when stdin is null, inherited stdin.

open_urls.description: Origins this capture command may open through nono's browser helper.

allow_launch_services.description: Mark this capture command as allowed to use host browser/app launch mechanisms during auth setup.

SecurityConfig.description: Security configuration for process-level isolation modes.

signal_mode.description: Signal isolation mode controlling whether the sandboxed process can signal other processes. When null, inherits from the base profile (defaults to isolated).

process_info_mode.description: Process inspection mode controlling whether the sandboxed process can read process info for other processes. When null, defaults to isolated.

ipc_mode.description: IPC mode controlling whether the sandboxed process can use POSIX semaphores (needed for multiprocessing). When null, defaults to shared_memory_only.

capability_elevation.description: Enable runtime capability elevation via seccomp-notify (Linux). When true, the supervisor intercepts file opens and can grant access to paths not in the initial capability set. When false, the sandbox is static.

wsl2_proxy_policy.description: WSL2 proxy fallback policy. Controls behavior when proxy-only network mode cannot be kernel-enforced on WSL2 (seccomp notify returns EBUSY). Default: error (refuse to run). Set to insecure_proxy to allow degraded execution where the credential proxy runs but network is not locked down.

Wsl2ProxyPolicy.description: WSL2 proxy fallback policy. 'error' (default) refuses to run if proxy-only mode cannot be kernel-enforced. 'insecure_proxy' allows degraded execution where the credential proxy injects credentials but the sandboxed process is not prevented from bypassing the proxy.

af_unix_mediation.description: Opt-in pathname AF_UNIX mediation. 'off' preserves compatibility. 'pathname' requires explicit filesystem.unix_socket* grants for pathname Unix socket connect/bind.

ProfileSignalMode.description: Signal isolation mode for the sandboxed process.

ProfileProcessInfoMode.description: Process inspection mode for the sandboxed process.

ProfileIpcMode.description: IPC mode for the sandboxed process. 'shared_memory_only' allows POSIX shared memory but denies semaphores. 'full' enables both shared memory and semaphores (required for Python multiprocessing and similar runtimes).

WhenPredicate.description: Platform predicate string or any-of array, e.g. linux, macos:>=15, linux:fedora-like:>=43, linux:fedora:>=43:workstation, or !linux:nixos.

FilesystemConfig.description: Filesystem access rules for the sandboxed process.

deny.description: Paths denied filesystem access. Denies apply regardless of other allow rules.

bypass_protection.description: Paths exempted from group-level deny rules. Each path must also appear in an allow/read/write section to actually grant access; bypass_protection only removes the deny.

suppress_save_prompt.description: Paths whose runtime denials should not be offered in the save-profile prompt. Does not grant access, remove deny rules, or hide diagnostic output.

ignore.description: Alias for suppress_save_prompt. Prefer filesystem.suppress_save_prompt in new profiles.

GroupsConfig.description: Group composition for the sandboxed process.

include.description: Policy group names to include. Groups are resolved against policy.json.

exclude.description: Policy group names to remove from the resolved group set (subtractive composition).

CommandsConfig.description: Command allow/deny lists applied during sandbox setup.

allow.description: Deprecated in v0.33.0. Startup-only command allowlist override. Not enforced for child processes; prefer resource-based controls instead.

deny.description: Deprecated in v0.33.0. Startup-only command denylist extension. Not enforced for child processes; prefer resource-based controls instead.

NetworkConfig.description: Network access configuration including proxy, credential injection, and port allowlists.

block.description: Block network access. Network is allowed by default; set to true to deny all outbound traffic.

allow_http2.description: Allow HTTP/2 to upstream servers via ALPN negotiation. Defaults to false (HTTP/1.1 with keep-alive). Equivalent to the --allow-http2 CLI flag.

network_profile.description: Network proxy profile name from network-policy.json. When set, outbound traffic is filtered through the proxy. Set to null to clear an inherited value.

allow_domain.description: Additional domains to allow through the proxy. Entries can be plain hostname strings (CONNECT tunnel, no L7 filtering) or objects with endpoint rules (TLS-intercepted method+path filtering). When endpoints are present, only matching requests are allowed (default-deny).

credentials.description: Credential service names to enable via the reverse proxy.

open_port.description: Localhost TCP IPC (connect+bind). Port 0: macOS only (localhost:* outbound); Linux needs explicit ports.

listen_port.description: TCP ports the sandboxed child may listen on.

custom_credentials.description: Custom credential definitions for services not in network-policy.json. Keys are service names used with --credential.

tls_intercept.description: TLS interception certificate lifecycle controls for L7 proxy routes.

upstream_proxy.description: Upstream proxy address (host:port) for enterprise proxy passthrough.

upstream_bypass.description: Hosts to bypass the upstream proxy and route directly. Supports exact hostnames and wildcard suffixes (e.g., *.example.com).

ca_lifecycle.description: CA lifecycle. session uses a per-run CA exposed through trust-bundle env vars. trusted reuses a CA trusted in the macOS user trust store.

ca_validity.description: CA validity duration, such as 24h or 1d. Bare numbers are seconds.

leaf_validity.description: Per-host leaf certificate validity duration, such as 15m. Leaf expiry is capped by CA expiry.

CustomCredentialDef.description: Custom credential route definition for the reverse proxy. Defines how to route requests and inject credentials for a service. Must have exactly one of: credential_key, auth, or aws_auth (mutually exclusive).

upstream.description: Upstream URL to proxy requests to (e.g., "https://api.telegram.org"). Must use HTTPS, or HTTP only for loopback addresses.

credential_key.description: Keystore account name for the credential (e.g., "telegram_bot_token"), or a 1Password op:// URI, Bitwarden bw:// URI, apple-password:// URI, file:// URI, env:// URI (e.g., "env://MY_TOKEN"), or cmd:// URI backed by credential_capture. Mutually exclusive with auth and aws_auth.

auth.description: OAuth2 client_credentials configuration. When present, the proxy handles token exchange automatically. Mutually exclusive with credential_key and aws_auth.

aws_auth.description: AWS SigV4 signing configuration. When present, the proxy signs outbound requests with AWS credentials. Mutually exclusive with credential_key and auth.

inject_mode.description: Credential injection mode. Determines how the credential is inserted into outbound requests.

inject_header.description: HTTP header name to inject the credential into. Only used when inject_mode is "header".

credential_format.description: Template with {} for the secret (header inject mode). If set, used as-is. If omitted: Authorization header (case-insensitive) defaults to Bearer {}; other headers default to {} (secret only).

path_pattern.description: Pattern to match in incoming URL path, using {} as placeholder for the phantom token. Required when inject_mode is "url_path".

path_replacement.description: Pattern for outgoing URL path, using {} as placeholder for the real credential. Defaults to path_pattern if not specified. Only used when inject_mode is "url_path".

query_param_name.description: Name of the query parameter to add or replace with the credential. Required when inject_mode is "query_param".

proxy.description: Optional proxy-side overrides for phantom token parsing. When omitted, proxy behavior uses top-level custom credential fields.

env_var.description: Explicit environment variable name for the phantom token (e.g., "OPENAI_API_KEY"). Required when credential_key is a URI manager reference (op://, bw://, apple-password://, or file://). Optional for env:// (derived from the URI).

endpoint_rules.description: L7 endpoint rules for method+path filtering. When non-empty, only requests matching at least one rule are allowed (default-deny). When empty or absent, all endpoints are permitted.

tls_ca.description: Path to a PEM-encoded CA certificate file for upstream TLS. When set, the proxy trusts this CA in addition to system roots. Required for upstreams with self-signed or private CA certificates (e.g. a Kubernetes API server). Supports tilde (~) expansion.

tls_client_cert.description: Path to a PEM-encoded client certificate for upstream mutual TLS (mTLS). When set together with tls_client_key, the proxy presents this certificate during the TLS handshake. Required for upstreams that enforce client-certificate authentication.

tls_client_key.description: Path to the PEM-encoded private key corresponding to tls_client_cert. Must be set together with tls_client_cert.

EndpointRule.description: HTTP method+path access rule for restricting which API endpoints an agent can access through a credential route.

method.description: HTTP method to match (e.g., "GET", "POST") or "*" for any method.

path.description: URL path glob pattern. Uses standard glob syntax: "*" matches one path segment, "**" matches zero or more segments.

AllowDomainWithEndpoints.description: Domain with fine-grained method+path endpoint restrictions. Requires TLS interception for L7 filtering. When endpoints is non-empty, only matching method+path combinations are allowed (default-deny).

endpoints.description: L7 method+path rules. Only requests matching at least one rule are allowed.

InjectMode.description: Credential injection mode for the reverse proxy.

ProxyInjectConfig.description: Optional proxy-side overrides for phantom token parsing. These settings only affect incoming client request validation, not outbound upstream credential injection.

inject_mode.description: Override inject mode for proxy-side phantom token parsing. Falls back to parent inject_mode when omitted.

inject_header.description: Override header name for proxy-side header/basic_auth modes. Falls back to parent inject_header when omitted.

credential_format.description: Override credential format for proxy-side header mode. Falls back to parent credential_format when omitted.

path_pattern.description: Override path pattern for proxy-side url_path mode. Falls back to parent path_pattern when omitted.

path_replacement.description: Override path replacement for proxy-side url_path mode. Falls back to parent path_replacement when omitted.

query_param_name.description: Override query parameter name for proxy-side query_param mode. Falls back to parent query_param_name when omitted.

OAuth2Config.description: OAuth2 client_credentials configuration for automatic token exchange. The proxy exchanges client_id + client_secret for an access_token, caches it, and refreshes automatically before expiry.

token_url.description: Token endpoint URL (e.g., "https://auth.example.com/oauth/token"). Must use HTTPS, or HTTP only for loopback addresses.

client_id.description: Client ID — plain value or credential reference (env://, file://, op://).

client_secret.description: Client secret — credential reference (env://, file://, op://).

scope.description: OAuth2 scopes (space-separated). Empty or omitted means no scope parameter is sent.

AwsAuthConfig.description: AWS SigV4 signing configuration for a credential route. All fields are optional: an empty {} block uses the default credential chain with region and service auto-detected from the upstream URL.

profile.description: AWS profile name to use for credentials. If omitted, the default credential chain is used (environment variables → AWS_PROFILE → default profile → instance metadata). Must be non-empty if set.

region.description: Explicit SigV4 signing region (e.g., "us-east-1"). If omitted, auto-detected from the upstream URL. Must be non-empty and contain no whitespace if set.

service.description: Explicit SigV4 service name (e.g., "bedrock", "s3"). If omitted, auto-detected from the upstream URL. Must be non-empty and contain no whitespace if set.

SecretsConfig.description: Maps keystore account names (or op://, bw://, apple-password://, env:// URIs) to environment variable names. Secrets are loaded from the system keystore under the service name "nono".

oneOf.description: Environment variable name to inject the secret value into.

env_var.description: Environment variable name to inject the secret value into.

EnvironmentConfig.description: Controls which environment variables are passed to the sandboxed process. By default all variables are inherited. Precedence (highest to lowest): 1. Hardcoded dangerous vars (always stripped), 2. deny_vars (stripped even if in allow_vars), 3. allow_vars (if non-empty, only matching vars pass).

allow_vars.description: Allow-list of environment variable names passed to the sandboxed process. Supports exact names ("PATH") and prefix patterns ending with * ("AWS_*"). When empty or absent, all variables are allowed.

deny_vars.description: Deny-list of environment variable names stripped from the sandboxed process. Supports exact names ("GH_TOKEN") and prefix patterns ending with * ("GITHUB_*"). Denied vars are stripped even if they also appear in allow_vars.

set_vars.description: Static environment variables injected after environment filtering and before credential injection. PATH and NONO_* are reserved. Values support variable expansion ($HOME, $WORKDIR, $TMPDIR, $XDG_*, $NONO_CONFIG, $NONO_PACKAGES).

executable_dirs.description: Additional directories searched during command target resolution before shim PATH is prepended.

allow_writable_executables.description: Explicit profile-wide trust downgrade that disables tool-sandbox checks for policy, deny-only, and outer executable allow-list paths writable through the sandbox capability set.

entrypoint.description: Legacy session entrypoint hint. Current profiles should express direct session access with commands.<name>.sandbox or commands.<name>.from.session.

approval_backends.description: Named approval backends used by approve decisions in invocation and endpoint policies.

approval_defaults.description: Default approval backend and timeout used when an approve decision does not route explicitly.

credentials.description: Named command credentials that may be injected only into selected child-tool-sandbox policies.

commands.description: Policy-controlled command definitions. A non-empty object activates tool-sandbox.

deny_direct_exec_bypass.description: Governance list of canonical executable files that child profiles may not re-grant through allow_direct_exec_bypass.

path.description: Path for raw-file credentials or local-socket credentials, commonly $SSH_AUTH_SOCK for SSH agent.

mode.description: Local socket access mode. Currently only connect is supported.

env_var.description: Environment variable mapped into the child. For local-socket this receives the resolved socket path; for proxy this receives the phantom token.

credential_key.description: Supervisor/proxy-side real credential reference. Supported forms include bare keyring account names and URI-backed sources such as env://, file://, keyring://, op://, bw://, and apple-password://.

base_url_env_var.description: Environment variable that points the child SDK/tool at the local proxy route.

inject_header.description: Upstream header used for proxy credential injection.

credential_format.description: Format string used when injecting the real proxy credential upstream.

source.description: Optional supervisor-side credential source for ambient credentials or proxy credentials. For proxy credentials, use either source or credential_key, not both.

tls_ca.description: Optional PEM CA bundle used by proxy credentials for upstream TLS verification.

tls_client_cert.description: Optional PEM client certificate for proxy credential mutual TLS. Must be paired with tls_client_key.

tls_client_key.description: Optional PEM client private key for proxy credential mutual TLS. Must be paired with tls_client_cert.

url.description: Webhook URL for webhook approval backends.

mode.description: Decision mode for chain approval backends.

backends.description: Child backend names for chain approval backends.

key.description: Credential reference resolved by the supervisor keystore backend.

command.description: Supervisor-side command used to print the credential on stdout.

args.description: Arguments passed to the supervisor-side credential command.

timeout_secs.description: Maximum time to wait for the credential command.

args.description: Argument prefix matched against argv[1..]. An empty list is a catch-all and must be last.

executable.description: Optional exact executable file for this command. When set, tool-sandbox binds the command name to this canonical file instead of resolving the first PATH match.

allow_writable_executable.description: Explicit per-command trust downgrade for a pinned executable or parent directory writable through the sandbox capability set. Valid only when executable is set to an absolute file path. On macOS this remains path-racy and is not suitable for high-assurance policies.

can_use.description: Other commands this command may invoke through tool-sandbox chaining. If no explicit self-invocation entry is configured, self-invocation keeps the command's current effective policy and does not require listing the command here.

sandbox.description: Direct from.session sandbox shorthand. Must not be combined with from.session.

from.description: Caller-specific policies. Use the reserved caller session for direct session invocation. Values may be "deny", a bare sandbox shorthand, or an edge object with sandbox and invocation_policy.

intercept.description: Ordered command argument-prefix intercept rules evaluated after invocation policy.

allow_direct_exec_bypass.description: Canonical executable paths allowed to bypass child-tool-sandbox when invoked directly, while PATH invocation remains shim-controlled.

allow_direct_exec_bypass_with_credential.description: Second opt-in required before a credential-using command may have direct exec bypass.

sandbox.description: Child sandbox selected for this caller edge.

invocation_policy.description: Optional argv and environment policy evaluated before launching the child command.

fs_read.description: Directory read grants for the selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

fs_read_file.description: Exact file read grants for the selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

fs_write.description: Directory write grants for the selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

fs_write_file.description: Exact file write grants for the selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

use_credentials.description: Credential handles injected into this selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

credentials.description: Credential handles injected into this selected child-tool-sandbox policy, optionally with endpoint policy constraints.

argv_prepend.description: Arguments inserted after the synthesized argv[0] and before shim-captured argv[1..] for this selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

network.description: Network grants for this selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

environment.description: Environment carry-over rules for this selected child-tool-sandbox policy.

allow_raw_file_credentials_in_chained_po.description: Explicit opt-in allowing raw-file credentials in a non-session caller policy.

resources.description: Optional child resource limits. Current runtimes fail closed if requested resources cannot be enforced.

stdio.description: Optional stdout/stderr byte limits enforced by brokered stdio.

open_urls.description: Runtime-delegated URL opening for this command (e.g. OAuth2 login). The unsandboxed tool-sandbox runtime validates each requested URL against allow_origins before opening it in the user's browser. When omitted, inherits from the base profile; when present, replaces it entirely.

allow_launch_services.description: macOS-only: allow this command to open URLs directly via LaunchServices instead of the origin-validated browser shim. Ignored on Linux. Defaults to false.

endpoint_policy.description: Optional method/path policy for proxy credentials.

allow_domain.description: Hostname egress allow-list. Valid only with an enforceable nono proxy/helper mode.

tcp_connect_ports.description: Raw TCP connect ports. Requires Landlock ABI v4+ TCP support and is not hostname-filtered.

tcp_bind_ports.description: Raw TCP bind ports. Requires Landlock ABI v4+ TCP support.

allow_vars.description: Authoritative environment variable allow-list for tool-sandbox. Supports exact names, trailing-prefix wildcards such as AWS_*, and a bare *.

set_vars.description: Static environment variables injected after tool-sandbox environment filtering and before credential injection. PATH and NONO_* are reserved.

access.description: Access level for the current working directory.

DiagnosticsConfig.description: Diagnostic output controls. Suppressions only affect nono's footer; the sandbox still denies the underlying operations.

suppress_system_services.description: Non-filesystem sandbox operation names to hide from diagnostic footers, such as forbidden-exec-sugid.

WorkdirAccess.description: Working directory access level controlling whether and how the current working directory is shared with the sandboxed process.

HooksConfig.description: Hook configurations for target applications. Keys are application names (e.g., "claude-code"), values define the hook to install.

HookConfig.description: Hook definition for a target application.

matcher.description: Regex pattern to match tool names (e.g., "Read|Write|Edit|Bash").

script.description: Script filename from data/hooks/ to install.

SessionHooks.description: Session lifecycle hooks. before runs before the sandboxed child is forked; after runs after it exits. Both run outside the sandbox with host privileges.

before.description: Hook executed before the sandboxed process starts. May export environment variables to the sandboxed child via NONO_ENV_FILE.

after.description: Hook executed after the sandboxed process exits. Receives the child exit code via NONO_EXIT_CODE.

SessionHook.description: A single session lifecycle hook configuration.

script.description: Absolute path to the hook script. Must be an executable regular file owned by the current user or root, not located in a world-writable directory. Validated at execution time.

timeout_secs.description: Optional timeout in seconds. If set, the hook is killed (SIGTERM then SIGKILL) after this duration. If absent, no timeout is enforced.

RollbackConfig.description: Rollback snapshot configuration controlling which files are excluded from undo snapshots.

exclude_patterns.description: Patterns to exclude from rollback snapshots. Matched against path components (exact match) or as substrings of the full path if they contain a slash.

exclude_globs.description: Glob patterns to exclude from rollback snapshots. Matched against the filename using standard glob syntax.

OpenUrlConfig.description: Configuration for supervisor-delegated URL opening, used for OAuth2 login flows and similar operations.

allow_origins.description: Allowed URL origins (scheme + host, e.g., "https://console.anthropic.com"). The supervisor validates each URL open request against this list. An empty list means no URLs are allowed.

allow_localhost.description: Allow opening http://localhost and http://127.0.0.1 URLs, typically for OAuth2 callback handlers.
